description: Global inter-agency registry listing principal needs and response indicators for humanitarian clusters, providing P-codes, definitions, search, filter and export functions.

framework_system: Global Reference List of 100 Core Health Indicators
description: Standard set of core health indicators prioritized by the global community to provide concise information on health situation and trends; includes four domains: health status, risk factors, service coverage and health systems. Guides selection of priority indicators and harmonises reporting across countries.

Guide for constructing and interpreting standard demographic and health survey indicators; assists countries in collecting and using data to monitor and evaluate population, health and nutrition programs.

MICS surveys gather internationally comparable data on the situation of children and women across multiple sectors; the MICS tools page provides questionnaires, indicator list and technical documentation for MICS7.

organization: Alliance for Child Protection in Humanitarian Action
framework_system: Minimum Standards for Child Protection in Humanitarian Action (CPMS) - Annex: Table of Indicators
description: Annex to the Minimum Standards for Child Protection in Humanitarian Action providing a measurement framework and indicators for each standard; updated in 2019 to support practitioners in generating and synthesizing evidence for child protection interventions.
Excel registry listing needs assessment and performance monitoring indicators for nutrition cluster interventions; provides standardized definitions and guidance for countries.

framework_system: Global WASH Cluster Assessment Indicator Bank
description: Bank of core indicators and questions for WASH needs assessments and monitoring; supports harmonized data collection across partners and standardized comparability.

Practical reference tool for WFP staff and partners providing methodologies and guidance to select and use indicators inside and outside WFP's Corporate Results Framework; serves as repository for indicator methodologies.

framework_system: Food Security Cluster Core Indicator Handbook 2017-2020
description: Handbook providing a core set of agreed-upon food security and livelihood indicators to support technical and non-technical members in designing and monitoring humanitarian food security interventions.

framework_system: Global Shelter Cluster Standardized Shelter Indicators
description: List of standardized shelter and settlement indicators to enable global tracking of shelter responses; links indicators to broader categories and Shelter Settlement Coordination pillars.

Iterative database developed by the Global Protection Cluster and Areas of Responsibility to guide selection of contextually relevant indicators for Humanitarian Needs Overviews; presents indicators across 15 protection risks, three pillars of needs and the Protection Analytical Framework; updated annually.

framework_system: GBV Minimum Standards for Prevention and Response in Emergencies - M&E Framework
description: Monitoring and evaluation framework complementing the GBV Minimum Standards; provides guidance and examples of core and recommended indicators to monitor achievement of each standard and compile best practices across agencies.

Indicator-based process assessing national migration governance across six dimensions; includes over 90 indicators operationalizing the Migration Governance Framework (MiGOF) to provide policy insights and help countries identify areas for improvement; not used for ranking.

Guidance note providing core indicators for preventing sexual exploitation and abuse (PSEA) across humanitarian responses, based on IASC PSEA acceleration plan and minimum actions; living document to track progress and ensure consistent measurement.

framework_system: Global Europe Results Framework (GERF) and EU Results Framework (EURF)
description: Corporate results frameworks used by the EU's DG INTPA to collect and measure key results achieved by interventions funded by external assistance; the EU Results Framework (EURF) introduced in 2015 and revised in 2018; the Global Europe Results Framework (GERF) introduced in 2022 for interventions funded under the NDICI 2021-2027; both include indicators and methodological notes accessible via Capacity4dev site.

sector: Multi-sector (Agriculture and Irrigation, Land, Roads, WASH, Education, Power)
description: Guidance document outlining common indicators used by MCC to standardize reporting across sectors; organizes indicators by process, output and outcome, ensuring cross-country comparison and mapping to program logic; instructs inclusion of exact wording and definition in M&E plans and indicator tracking tables.

framework_system: Guidance on the Indicator Tracking Table
description: Guidance detailing the Indicator Tracking Table (ITT), a quarterly reporting tool for MCC programs; explains ITT purpose, format, data inputs, submission cycle, and its role in tracking progress of indicators included in M&E plans.

framework_system: Monitoring, Evaluation, and Reporting (MER) Indicator Reference Guide
description: Comprehensive reference guide for PEPFAR's MER indicators, providing definitions, calculation methods, and reporting guidance for partners; includes harmonization with UNAIDS and Global Fund reporting.

organization: Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)
description: Standardised experience-based metric for measuring access to food; used for monitoring SDG 2.1.2; provides survey modules, methodologies and analytic guidelines.

framework_system: Joint Monitoring Programme (JMP) Core Questions and Indicators
description: Collection of core indicators and questions used by the JMP to monitor progress on WASH in households, schools and health care facilities, aligned with SDG targets; accessible via data portal.

framework_system: Indicators for assessing infant and young child feeding practices
description: Updated set of indicators and measurement guidance for assessing infant and young child feeding practices.

Global set of indicators adopted by the UN General Assembly to monitor progress toward the Sustainable Development Goals; includes 247 unique indicators across 17 goals with metadata and periodic updates.

framework_system: Counter-Trafficking in Persons (CTIP) MEL Indicator Library
description: IndiKit-hosted library of standardized monitoring, evaluation and learning (MEL) indicators for counter-trafficking in persons programs; provides indicator definitions, data collection guidance, and measurement tools developed for USAID-funded implementers working on CTIP programming.

Curated library of indicators for measuring knowledge management performance across organizations; provides metrics covering knowledge capture, sharing, transfer and organizational learning — useful for M&E practitioners embedding KM into results frameworks.
Practitioner-oriented online resource library of M&E indicators organised by sector and theme; provides indicator definitions and measurement guidance for development and humanitarian programs.

UNICEF MENA region framework providing minimum quality standards and indicators for child-focused program implementation; sets benchmarks and measurement guidance to ensure consistent quality across programmatic areas in the Middle East and North Africa.

organization: Inter-agency Network for Education in Emergencies (INEE)
description: Toolkit accompanying the INEE Minimum Standards for Education in Emergencies; includes indicators, self-assessment checklists and guidance notes to support practitioners in monitoring compliance with minimum standards across preparedness, response and recovery phases.

framework_system: UN iLibrary Statistics and Indicators Database
description: UN iLibrary statistical and indicator database providing access to data, statistical publications and indicator series from across the UN system, covering economic, social, environmental and demographic dimensions.

framework_system: Minimum Quality Standards and Indicators for Community Engagement
description: Guide providing minimum quality standards and a set of indicators for measuring community engagement in child health programs; supports implementers in designing, monitoring and evaluating the quality and reach of community-level health interventions.

organization: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
description: UNECE technical document presenting indicators for measuring alternative care systems for children, including foster care, kinship care and residential care; supports countries in monitoring compliance with international standards and improving care quality.
